email.description: The email address that was verified.

status.description: Overall verdict. valid = mailbox accepts mail; invalid = will bounce; risky = deliverable but low-confidence (catch-all, role, or major-provider that lies at SMTP); unknown = could not be determined (timeout/block). Unknown results are not charged.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason code, e.g. bad_syntax, no_mx, mailbox_not_found, catch_all, disposable, role_account, provider_unverifiable, smtp_timeout, smtp_blocked.

score.description: Deterministic 0-100 deliverability score derived from the checks. Higher is safer to send to.

mxFound.description: Whether the domain has at least one valid MX record.

isDisposable.description: Domain is a known disposable/throwaway provider.

isRoleAccount.description: Local part is a role address (info, sales, admin, support, etc.) rather than a person.

isFreeProvider.description: Domain is a free consumer provider (gmail, outlook, yahoo, etc.).

isCatchAll.description: Domain accepts mail for any address, so a positive SMTP result is not conclusive.
